channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander, Jokic play to a draw in back-to-back games between top MVP candidates
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Nikola Jokic, two of the favorites for the NBA's MVP award, showed why in a pair of exciting games on back-to-back days in Oklahoma City. Neither emerged as a clear favorite to claim the hardware.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: After the milestone was first wiped out by an offsides review, MacKinnon finally earns 1,000th point
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Nathan MacKinnon appeared to have reached the 1,000-point milestone in the first period of Colorado's game against Chicago. His teammates even streamed onto the ice to celebrate his history-making assist. That turned out to be just a dress rehearsal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Josh Giddey scores season-high 29 points before injuring ankle and Bulls beat Pacers 121-103
item.description: Josh Giddey had a season-high 29 points along with 10 rebounds before leaving midway through the fourth quarter with a right ankle injury, and the Chicago Bulls won their third straight game, 121-103 over the Indiana Pacers on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: QB Sam Darnold agrees on a 3-year, $100.5 million deal with Seahawks, AP source says
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks found the replacement for Geno Smith, agreeing to a three-year, $100.5 million contract with Sam Darnold, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sam Hauser becomes 4th Celtics player with 9 3s in a game as Boston keeps shooting from deep
item.description: Sam Hauser started in place of Jayson Tatum and did what he and the Boston Celtics have done all season — shoot a bunch of 3-pointers and make more than their share.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Mcgair, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jazz losing skid hits six after 114-108 loss at Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) - Sam Hauser made nine 3-pointers — seven in the third quarter — and finished with a career-high 33 points to lead the short-handed Boston Celtics past the Utah Jazz 114-108 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets and QB Justin Fields agree to a 2-year, $40 million deal, AP source says
item.description: Justin Fields was passed over by the New York Jets four years ago in the NFL draft. Now he's expected to be their No. 1 quarterback. Fields and the Jets agreed Monday to a two-year contract worth $40 million, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The 49ers lose several key players on the opening day of free agency
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The opening of free agency for the San Francisco 49ers was more about their losses than their additions.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bills' Josh Allen agrees to 6-year extension with record-setting guarantee: reports
item.description: Josh Allen, fresh off an MVP campaign, just got paid as the Buffalo Bills announced a six-year extension, which reportedly is worth $330 million with $250 million guaranteed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daytona 500 winner William Byron claims NASCAR Cup pole
item.description: On Saturday, Daytona 500 winner William Byron claimed the Busch Light pole during the 312-mile race at Phoenix Raceway. According to NASCAR, it's the 14th pole of the 27 year-old's career.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utes hire Alex Jensen as head basketball coach
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Alex Jensen is coming home to Utah. Jensen was hired as the University of Utah's 17th head basketball coach Thursdaay, replacing Craig Smith, who was fired last month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Yanni Gourde back where he belongs, in Tampa Bay
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning organization believes that adding two elite forwards Yanni Gourde and Oliver Byorkstrand will be the push they need to make a deep playoff run.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Special Olympics Illinois athletes head to Italy for snowshoe races
item.description: On Wednesday, a team of Special Olympics Illinois snowshoe athletes boarded a plane to New York, with a final destination of Turin, Italy. There they will compete in the Special Olympics Winter World Games.
item.dc:creator: Erin McElroy, NewsNation

item.title: Goalie of the future? Spencer Knight makes 41 saves, backstops Blackhawks to win over Kings in debut with team
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN, AP) — Spencer Knight was once considered the goaltender of the future for one NHL franchise. Now he plans to make a case as the goaltender of the future for another, and he got right to work with the Blackhawks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
